source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansible Intermediate: Managing changes: the handlers

options: A notify from a task that reported a change
questions.why: Tasks notify handlers; a handler reacts to a change, e.g. restarting a service.

questions.q: When does a notified handler actually run?
options: After all the tasks in the block have completed
Immediately, the moment it is notified
Never, unless forced with a flag
questions.why: Handlers wait until all tasks finish, then run.

questions.q: If several tasks notify the same handler, how many times does it run?
questions.why: A handler runs only once even if notified multiple times, avoiding needless restarts.

questions.q: Since Ansible 2.2, how can several handlers respond to one notification topic?
options: With the `listen` keyword, e.g. listen: "web services restart"
questions.why: Handlers can listen to a shared topic; notifying that topic runs every listener.

questions.q: A module is idempotent, so what lets a playbook fire a handler?
options: The task detected a significant change on the remote system
It fires on every single run regardless
A manual flag passed each run
questions.why: When a task reports a change, its notify triggers the handler.
